paper_title: Linking Marine Plankton Ecosystems and Climate: A New Modeling Approach to the Warm Early Eocene Climate

methods.description: The cGENIE Earth System Model is used to explore interactions between marine biogeochemistry and climate over various timescales and periods, including the Eocene and PETM. It integrates a three-dimensional ocean circulation model, an atmospheric energy-moisture balance model, and a sea-ice model to simulate biogeochemical transformations and distributions.

steps.description: Employ the carbon-centric version of the GENIE Earth system model of intermediate complexity: cGENIE.
steps.searchable_summary: use cGENIE model for Earth system simulations

steps.description: The physical model of cGENIE consists of a three-dimensional non-eddy resolving frictional geostrophic ocean circulation model, coupled to a two-dimensional energy-moisture balance model of the atmosphere and a dynamic-thermodynamic sea-ice model.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate ocean circulation, atmosphere, and sea-ice dynamics

steps.description: The biogeochemistry model represents the transformations and redistribution of essential biogeochemical compounds and isotopes via biological processes.
Model parameters are calibrated using modern global annual mean observations.

methods.description: EcoGEnIE is an ecological model integrated into cGENIE that explicitly resolves plankton communities, considering nutrient and light availability, resource competition, and grazing by zooplankton. It models the production and export of organic matter based on size-structured plankton populations.

steps.description: Replace the standard biogeochemical model in cGENIE with EcoGEnIE, which explicitly resolves the plankton community.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate EcoGEnIE model for plankton community simulation

steps.description: Resolve plankton populations by functional group and organism size, with biomass state variables for carbon, phosphorus, and chlorophyll.
Model nutrient uptake by phytoplankton using a Michaelis-Menten-like function, with size-dependent maximum uptake rate and nutrient affinity.
Simulate zooplankton grazing based on prey biomass concentration and size-dependent maximum grazing rate.
Model the production and export of organic matter as a function of plankton mortality and inefficient biomass assimilation by zooplankton.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate organic matter production and export
